subject: object-oriented development
claim: Aberdeen believes the time to implement object technology is now, when combined with appropriate levels of training and support

subject: IS strategy
claim: Successful IS executives achieve competitive objectives by developing flexible applications that model core organizational activities

subject: OO benefits
claim: Aberdeen's field research consistently shows OO development delivers real long-term benefits but only with the right mix of advanced tools and training

subject: OO applications
claim: Object-oriented software is inherently modular resulting in better-organized code where problems and elements are more quickly isolated

subject: legacy integration
claim: Object-oriented applications can integrate existing software by building an interface wrapper around legacy systems, preserving previous software investments

subject: distributed computing
claim: An industry transition to distributed computing is underway driven by geographically distributed business units and the need to share core databases

subject: enterprise application management
claim: When a business rule changes enterprise-wide it will become cost-prohibitive to have programming staff hand-code all occurrences without integrated distributed software methods

subject: OO adoption success factors
claim: Aberdeen field research consistently shows that when any one of four critical factors is missing (robust tools / training / implementation expertise / leadership vendor) there is a much higher chance of delays, cost overruns, and user dissatisfaction

subject: Hewlett-Packard
claim: In Aberdeen's opinion, Hewlett-Packard has the right combination of products, services and partnerships needed in today's market

subject: C++ SoftBench 5.0
claim: SoftBench has 80-plus third-party tools encapsulated into HP's development environment covering the entire software development lifecycle from analysis and design to testing and maintenance

subject: SoftBench Encapsulator
claim: Using the Encapsulator, SoftBench embeds third-party tools so they share the same user interface and seamlessly communicate with each other enabling best-of-breed tool selection

subject: HP-UX and Solaris
claim: SoftBench supports not only HP-UX but also runs on Sun Solaris

subject: SoftBench static analyzer and debugger
claim: SoftBench's static analyzer and debugger have gotten particularly high marks from developers interviewed by Aberdeen

subject: SoftBench visualization
claim: SoftBench graphically depicts application structure and object inheritance, shortening the time needed for new developers to learn object technology

subject: Graphical Class Editor
claim: New SoftBench 5.0 Graphical Class Editor allows developers to visually edit objects with changes automatically reflected in underlying C++ code by making static analysis database read-write

subject: CodeAdvisor
claim: Aberdeen believes CodeAdvisor represents an elegant way to teach new C++ developers the subtleties of OO programming and will significantly accelerate object adoption timetables

subject: CodeAdvisor
claim: CodeAdvisor automatically enforces C++ coding rules by using the static analysis database to discover common coding errors such as improper object copying with dynamically allocated memory

subject: SoftBench CM
claim: SoftBench CM supports version control, checkin/checkout, audit trails, change histories and access control and allows parallel work across multiple HP-UX and Solaris servers

subject: multi-language support
claim: For systems mixing C++, C and COBOL, HP offers COBOL SoftBench and COBOL/C++ SoftBench enabling incremental migration from procedural to OO programming

subject: database support
claim: SoftBench 5.0 supports Informix, DB2/9000, Oracle, Sybase and Allbase databases along with Micro Focus COBOL and CICS

subject: distributed development
claim: SoftBench CM enables developers to collaborate on a project even when distributed across an enterprise

subject: DCE integration
claim: SoftBench can build applications that use OSF's Distributed Computing Environment enabling software modules to execute on multiple networked hardware platforms

subject: SoftBench overall
claim: Aberdeen believes SoftBench's team development features and ability to encapsulate third-party tools result in an excellent environment for professional developers building mission-critical applications

subject: legacy modernization
claim: HP's experience shows customers first inventory existing applications to determine what should be encapsulated and linked to new OO applications using C++ SoftBench and UIM/X

subject: OO migration
claim: Companies moving to object technology face two major challenges: development staff retraining and migrating the code base

subject: object reuse
claim: Third-party libraries of generic pre-built objects and templates are used to provide base-level functionality reducing development time

subject: SoftBench Training
claim: HP offers a two-day basic bootcamp teaching SoftBench fundamentals tailored to programming languages and organizational needs

subject: Distributed Object Consulting
claim: HP provides focused on-site consulting helping clients design and implement distributed OO applications with project management expertise

subject: Object-Oriented Solutions Center
claim: HP's Object-Oriented Solutions Center provides IS management organizational and business-related assistance including hardware platform selection, network design and software licensing

subject: Transition Services
claim: HP assesses and provides services for organizational and training needs accompanying the transition to object technology

subject: HP OODCE
claim: HP provides Object-Oriented Distributed Computing Environment (OODCE) used in conjunction with SoftBench to create applications running on OSF DCE

subject: HP Distributed Smalltalk
claim: HP Distributed Smalltalk (DST) allows building CORBA-compliant Smalltalk systems conforming to OMG CORBA specification using an object request broker

subject: HP ORB Plus
claim: In the first half of 1996 HP plans to roll out ORB Plus to let developers create CORBA-compliant C++ applications with C++ SoftBench

subject: distributed computing trend
claim: Aberdeen firmly believes distributed computing will become increasingly critical to daily business operations and user organizations should evaluate a supplier's distributed computing strategy even with no immediate plans

subject: SoftBench future
claim: HP's vision is applications assembled from pre-built components from internal development, third parties, or legacy systems; SoftBench will gain enhanced graphical programming and code maintenance capabilities

subject: SoftBench static analysis history
claim: The static analysis database added in September 1994 SoftBench 4.0 rollout is the key underpinning for the newly-released SoftBench C++ CodeAdvisor and Graphical Editor demonstrating HP's forethought

subject: CommonPoint integration
claim: HP is porting Taligent's CommonPoint framework to HP-UX; SoftBench can modify CommonPoint C++ components resulting in customized applications for collaborative desktop use

subject: CommonPoint adoption
claim: Aberdeen believes more object-adept SoftBench users will soon want to use CommonPoint to build collaborative desktop applications running on a wide range of hardware platforms

subject: C++ SoftBench
claim: Aberdeen believes C++ SoftBench deserves serious attention from organizations contemplating a transition to object-oriented software development

subject: Hewlett-Packard financials
claim: HP is viewed as a trusted supplier with $25 billion in revenues having the resources to take on the largest and most complex assignments

subject: IS transformation
claim: Aberdeen believes organizations that realize the impact of the change in application development and take advantage of SoftBench's advanced features will be the first to turn information systems from a cost center into a revenue engine

subject: OO adoption drivers
claim: The heavy use of objects by independent software vendors for developing products is accelerating the adoption of OO technology

subject: new development environment
claim: A new development environment must include robust browsers, editors, compilers, support team development, and cover the full software lifecycle from analysis and design to testing

subject: QuickFix
claim: QuickFix automates the debugging process as one of the new features in SoftBench 5.0

subject: ToolBar customization
claim: SoftBench 5.0 includes a customizable color ToolBar for frequently used activities improving developer workflow
